intentional_test_features: Elderly Type 2 diabetic nursing home resident with insidious dehydration and progressive confusion over a week, extreme hyperglycaemia, but no Kussmaul breathing or ketotic breath — tests recognition of hyperosmolar hyperglycaemic state (HHS) as distinct from DKA

Tests whether the AI Doctor recognises that the absence of classic DKA features (Kussmaul breathing, acetone breath) does not mean this is less dangerous — HHS carries a higher mortality rate and requires urgent, carefully-paced IV fluid resuscitation
Tests recognition of a possible untreated UTI as a precipitant requiring identification and treatment alongside the metabolic emergency

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-01)
